vulnerabilities.type: API Keys and Secrets Exposed in Version Control
vulnerabilities.title: Production Secrets Committed to Git Repository
vulnerabilities.description: Multiple .env files (.env.local, .env.vps, .env.sentry) contain production credentials and are tracked in git history. These files are in .gitignore but may have been committed before the rule was added. Active secrets include database credentials, API keys for Twilio, Telegram, Google, Anthropic, Meta, Stripe, and Vercel tokens.
vulnerabilities.impact: CRITICAL - Attackers can: execute arbitrary API calls, send SMS/Telegram messages impersonating business, access entire PostgreSQL database, hijack admin accounts, manipulate Stripe payments, deploy malicious code to Vercel
vulnerabilities.current_status: .env files are in .gitignore but may exist in git history
immediate_actions: 1. IMMEDIATELY rotate ALL exposed secrets (call third parties: Twilio, Meta, Google, Stripe)
2. Check git history: git log --all -p -- .env.local | head -50
4. Force push: git push origin --force --all (after filtering history)
5. Notify GitHub Security to scan for leaks
6. Regenerate all tokens in Vercel Environment Variables
remediation: Install pre-commit hook to prevent secret commits: npm install husky pre-commit-secrets
Use Vercel Environment Variables (already configured, just use it exclusively)
Store development secrets in local .env.local (not committed)
Use GitHub Secrets for CI/CD workflows
Implement GitGuardian or TruffleHog for scanning
Document secret rotation policy

vulnerabilities.type: DOM XSS via dangerouslySetInnerHTML Without Sanitization
vulnerabilities.title: Unescaped HTML in Email Body Display
vulnerabilities.description: Email body content is rendered using dangerouslySetInnerHTML without HTML sanitization in the conversations page. Email content stored in database (email_logs table) could contain malicious HTML/JavaScript, either through email parsing bugs or database compromise. An attacker could inject
vulnerabilities.attack_vector: Attacker sends email with payload ''. Email is parsed and stored in database. Admin views conversation → payload executes → session cookie stolen.
vulnerabilities.impact: HIGH - Account takeover (admin session hijacking), credential theft, unauthorized admin actions, exfiltration of all customer quotes/invoices/leads

proof_of_concept.trigger: Send email containing payload → click conversation in CRM dashboard

remediation: Install DOMPurify: npm install dompurify @types/dompurify
Update conversations/page.tsx line 153:
import DOMPurify from 'dompurify';
<div dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: DOMPurify.sanitize(e.body ?? '') }} className="prose prose-invert max-w-none" />
Alternatively, use react-markdown with HTML disabled
Add Content-Security-Policy header: 'script-src \'self\'; object-src \'none\''
test_cases: Test 2: - should be sanitized
Test 4: <a href="javascript:alert('XSS')"> - should have href removed

vulnerabilities.title: Missing Authorization Checks on Quote/Invoice Endpoints
vulnerabilities.description: Multiple API endpoints accept dynamic IDs without verifying the authenticated user has permission to access that resource. For example, GET /api/quotes/[id] retrieves quote details for ANY quote ID if user is authenticated, without checking if the quote belongs to the user's organization.

affected_endpoints.issue: Returns full quote details without checking ownership
Returns invoice details without permission check
Modifies ANY quote without ownership verification
Returns client details without checking access

vulnerabilities.attack_vector: An authenticated user (or attacker with valid session) iterates through resource IDs: 1, 2, 3, 4... to enumerate and read/modify all quotes, invoices, clients, expenses in system regardless of ownership.
vulnerabilities.impact: CRITICAL - Unauthorized access to all customer data: quotes with pricing, invoices with payment info, client contact details, business decisions, work schedules

proof_of_concept.result: Returns quote details for any ID without verifying ownership
proof_of_concept.escalation: Loop through IDs to dump all quotes in system

remediation: Add ownership check before returning resource:
const quote = await query('SELECT * FROM quotes WHERE id = $1', [parseInt(id)]);
if (!quote[0]) return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Not found' }, { status: 404 });
// Verify ownership (assuming quotes belong to current user/org)
if (quote[0].owner_id !== session?.user?.id) {
return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Forbidden' }, { status: 403 });
Use UUIDs instead of sequential IDs to reduce enumeration
Implement row-level security (RLS) in PostgreSQL
vulnerabilities.implementation_priority: IMMEDIATE - affects all sensitive data endpoints

vulnerabilities.title: DoS Vulnerability in AI SDK Dependencies
vulnerabilities.description: npm audit identifies GHSA-866g-f22w-33x8 affecting @ai-sdk/provider-utils versions <= 3.0.97. This causes Uncontrolled Resource Consumption (DoS) attacks. The vulnerability affects ai, @ai-sdk/anthropic, @ai-sdk/react, and related packages used in chat endpoints.

vulnerable_endpoints.impact: DoS via crafted requests to chat endpoint

vulnerabilities.attack_vector: Attacker sends specially crafted requests to /api/chat that cause unbounded resource consumption, leading to server exhaustion and service downtime.
vulnerabilities.impact: HIGH - Denial of Service: chat endpoint becomes unavailable, server resources exhausted
remediation: Update packages: npm install ai@6.0.202+ (includes fixed @ai-sdk/provider-utils)
Run npm audit fix --force if needed (may have breaking changes)
Add timeout limits to chat endpoint: requestTimeout: 30000 (30 seconds max)
Implement per-user rate limiting: max 10 requests/minute per user
Add max body size limit: maxBodySize: 1MB
Monitor CPU/memory during LLM calls, implement circuit breaker pattern
Test: npm audit 2>&1 | grep GHSA-866g-f22w-33x8 (should return empty)
vulnerabilities.verification: npm audit should show zero high/critical vulnerabilities after fix

vulnerabilities.title: URL Parsing Vulnerabilities in fast-uri Package
vulnerabilities.description: fast-uri <= 3.1.1 has two vulnerabilities: GHSA-q3j6-qgpj-74h6 (path traversal) and GHSA-v39h-62p7-jpjc (host confusion). These affect URL parsing throughout the application.
vulnerabilities.impact: HIGH - Path traversal attacks, SSRF (Server-Side Request Forgery) if URLs are used for external requests
remediation: Update fast-uri to >= 3.2.0: npm install fast-uri@latest
Validate all user-supplied URLs: try { new URL(userInput); } catch { return 400; }
Use URL allowlist for external API calls:
const ALLOWED_DOMAINS = ['api.meta.com', 'api.twilio.com', 'api.anthropic.com'];
if (!ALLOWED_DOMAINS.some(d => url.includes(d))) return 400;

vulnerabilities.title: Cross-Site Scripting in jsondiffpatch Package
vulnerabilities.description: jsondiffpatch < 0.7.2 has XSS vulnerability (GHSA-33vc-wfww-vjfv) via HtmlFormatter. If used to render JSON diffs as HTML, attacker-controlled JSON could execute JavaScript.
vulnerabilities.impact: HIGH - XSS if jsondiffpatch HtmlFormatter is used in UI
remediation: Update jsondiffpatch to >= 0.7.2
If using HtmlFormatter, sanitize output with DOMPurify
Consider using text-based diff rendering instead of HTML

vulnerabilities.type: Weak API Key Validation Without Rate Limiting
vulnerabilities.title: Static API Key Comparison in Multiple Endpoints
vulnerabilities.description: API endpoints validate admin access using simple string comparison against ADMIN_API_KEY environment variable without rate limiting or additional security measures. The API key is plaintext in environment variables and exposed in version control.
vulnerabilities: No rate limiting on failed API key attempts
No API key expiration or rotation mechanism
Single API key used for multiple services with different scopes
No audit logging of API key usage
vulnerabilities.impact: HIGH - If API key is compromised, attacker has full admin access without rate limiting
remediation: Rotate ADMIN_API_KEY immediately
Implement API key hashing: store hash(key) in database, compare hash(provided)
Add API key rotation with expiration dates (e.g., 90 days)
Implement per-endpoint scoping: separate keys for read/write/delete operations
Add rate limiting: max 100 requests/minute per API key
Implement HMAC-SHA256 request signing: HMAC(body + timestamp, secret)
Add comprehensive audit logging for all API key usage
Move API key validation to middleware.ts for consistent protection

vulnerabilities.title: Admin Password Stored as Plaintext
vulnerabilities.description: ADMIN_PASSWORD is stored as plaintext in environment variables. While the authentication code supports bcrypt comparison, storing plaintext is a security anti-pattern. If .env is exposed, passwords are immediately compromised without bcrypt cost factor protection.
vulnerabilities.impact: HIGH - Plaintext password compromise, exposed in version control
remediation: Generate bcrypt hash: npm install -g bcryptjs; node -e "console.log(require('bcryptjs').hashSync('password', 10))"
Replace ADMIN_PASSWORD plaintext with bcrypt hash in .env
Update AUTHORIZED_USERS format to use only bcrypt hashes
Remove plaintext password support from auth.ts after migration
Document bcrypt hash generation in CLAUDE.md

vulnerabilities.title: User Input in PDF Template Without Proper Escaping
vulnerabilities.description: PDF rendering uses Puppeteer to generate quotes/invoices. User-controlled data (client names, quote descriptions, etc.) is injected into the PDF template without HTML entity encoding. This could allow JavaScript injection during PDF generation.
vulnerabilities.attack_vector: Attacker submits quote with client_nom containing JavaScript: "". PDF generation executes JavaScript in Puppeteer context.
vulnerabilities.impact: HIGH - Information disclosure from Puppeteer context, potential RCE if process runs with elevated privileges
remediation: Escape all user-controlled data in PDF templates with HTML entity encoding:
const escapeHtml = (text) => text.replace(/[&<>"']/g, char => ({ '&': '&', '<': '<', '>': '>', '"': '"', "'": ''' }[char]));
Use template engine with auto-escaping (Handlebars): npm install handlebars
Add input validation: quote.client_nom.replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9\s-]/g, '')
Sandbox Puppeteer: use --disable-dev-shm-usage --no-sandbox (still prefer proper escaping)
Review and test all PDF templates (invoice-pdf.ts, contract-pdf.ts, etc.)

vulnerabilities.title: Payment/Verification Tokens Exposed in URL Query Strings
vulnerabilities.description: Secret tokens (secret_token) are passed in URL query parameters for payment pages and contract verification. URLs are logged in browser history, server access logs, proxies, and referrer headers, exposing tokens indefinitely.
logged_locations: Browser history (accessible to any device user)
Email log files if link is forwarded
vulnerabilities.impact: HIGH - Long-term token exposure, unauthorized payment/contract access if token is intercepted
remediation: Use POST requests with tokens in request body instead of URL params
Implement short-lived, single-use tokens (5-minute expiration)
Add Referrer-Policy: no-referrer header to payment pages
Use authenticated sessions instead of bearer tokens in URLs
Log token access attempts and rotate tokens regularly
Implement rate limiting on payment endpoint
Example: POST /api/invoices/{id}/payment with { token } in JSON body

vulnerabilities.title: Raw Error Details in API Responses
vulnerabilities.description: Some API endpoints return raw error objects or exception details that reveal system internals: database structure, file paths, dependency versions, etc.
vulnerabilities.vulnerable_code: return NextResponse.json({ error: `Erreur envoi email: ${String(err)}` }, { status: 500 });
vulnerabilities.impact: MEDIUM-HIGH - Information gathering for attack planning
remediation: Return generic error messages to clients
Log detailed errors server-side only (for debugging)
Example:
try { ... } catch (err) {
console.error('Email send failed:', err);  // log detailed error
return NextResponse.json(
{ error: 'Failed to send email. Please try again.' },
{ status: 500 }
Never return err.toString(), err.message, or err.stack to client

vulnerabilities.title: Full Client Information Returned Without Filtering
vulnerabilities.description: API endpoints return full client records including email, phone, and address without filtering. Violates principle of least privilege.
vulnerabilities.impact: MEDIUM - PII exposure to authenticated users
remediation: Return only necessary fields: name for list view, full contact only in detail view
Implement field-level access control based on user role
Mask PII in list views: 'test...@example.com' instead of full email

vulnerabilities.title: File Extension Validation Relies on Content-Type Header
vulnerabilities.description: File upload endpoint validates extension primarily through Content-Type header which is controlled by client. Path.basename() used for safety but doesn't fully prevent attacks.
vulnerabilities: File extension not validated against allowlist
Content-Type header is client-controlled and can be spoofed
No magic bytes verification (file content signature)
Fallback to local filesystem is risky
vulnerabilities.impact: MEDIUM - Malicious file upload (web shell, malware)
remediation: Validate file extensions against whitelist: const ALLOWED = ['.jpg', '.png', '.gif', '.webp']
Use magic bytes verification instead of Content-Type: npm install file-type
Verify actual file type: const type = await fileType.fromBuffer(fileBuffer); if (type?.mime !== 'image/...')
Use random UUIDs for filenames, discard original names
Store uploads in Vercel Blob only, never local filesystem
Implement antivirus scanning: npm install clamav.js or use external API
Set Content-Disposition: attachment to prevent browser execution

vulnerabilities.type: Missing CSRF Protection on State-Changing Operations
vulnerabilities.title: Form Actions Not Protected Against CSRF
vulnerabilities.description: State-changing endpoints (POST/PUT/DELETE) may not have adequate CSRF protection. While Next.js provides built-in CSRF tokens, explicit validation is unclear.
vulnerabilities.issue: No explicit CSRF token validation in API routes
vulnerabilities.impact: MEDIUM - Attacker could trick admin into performing actions via malicious website
remediation: Verify Next.js CSRF protection is enabled (default in Next.js 13+)
Explicitly validate CSRF tokens in API routes if needed
Add SameSite=Strict to session cookies
Validate Origin/Referer headers for sensitive operations
Example: const origin = req.headers.get('origin'); if (origin !== 'https://novus-epoxy.vercel.app') return 403;

vulnerabilities.type: Insufficient Rate Limiting on Public Endpoints
vulnerabilities.title: API Rate Limiting May Be Inadequate
vulnerabilities.description: Public endpoints like /api/track and /api/submissions have rate limiting in middleware, but per-user rate limits on authenticated endpoints may be insufficient.
vulnerabilities.impact: MEDIUM - Brute force attacks (password guessing, token enumeration)
remediation: Implement per-user rate limiting on auth endpoints: max 5 failed login attempts/minute
Implement per-user rate limiting on API endpoints: max 100 requests/minute
Add progressive delays on failed attempts (exponential backoff)
Monitor and alert on rate limit violations
npm install rate-limiter-flexible or express-rate-limit

vulnerabilities.title: Limited Audit Logging of Security Events
vulnerabilities.description: While login attempts are logged, other security events (API key usage, failed authorization, data access) may not be comprehensively logged.
vulnerabilities.impact: MEDIUM - Reduced ability to detect and investigate security incidents
remediation: Add audit logging for all security events:
- Login attempts (success/failure)
- API key usage
- Authorization failures (IDOR attempts)
- Data access (who accessed what resource)
- Configuration changes
- Permission changes
Create audit_logs table with: id, action, user, resource, timestamp, ip, result
Implement log aggregation (Sentry, LogRocket, or similar)
Set up alerts for suspicious patterns

vulnerabilities.title: Security Headers Not Fully Configured
vulnerabilities.description: Some security headers are set but may be incomplete. CSP (Content-Security-Policy) is not configured, allowing inline scripts.
missing_headers: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff - prevents MIME type sniffing
vulnerabilities.impact: MEDIUM - Reduces defense-in-depth against XSS and other attacks
remediation: Add CSP header: 'Content-Security-Policy': "default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; object-src 'none'"
Add X-Content-Type-Options: 'nosniff'
Add X-Frame-Options: 'DENY'
Add X-XSS-Protection: '1; mode=block'
Add Strict-Transport-Security: 'max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains'
Test with: curl -I https://novus-epoxy.vercel.app | grep -i security

vulnerabilities.title: Potential Debug Code Left in Production
vulnerabilities.description: Code may contain commented-out debugging statements or TODOs that could reveal implementation details.
vulnerabilities.impact: LOW - Information disclosure during code review
remediation: Remove all commented-out code before commits
Use GitHub pre-commit hooks to prevent commit of debug code
Enforce in CLAUDE.md: 'No commented-out code in commits'

immediate: SEC-002: Sanitize email body HTML output (XSS)
SEC-003: Add authorization checks to resource endpoints (IDOR)
short_term: SEC-005 through SEC-010: Address remaining HIGH severity issues
SEC-007: Implement API key rate limiting
medium_term: SEC-011 through SEC-018: Address MEDIUM and LOW severity issues
Implement API key hashing and rotation

compliance_notes.gdpr: PII exposure (SEC-012) violates data minimization principle
compliance_notes.pci_dss: Stripe token handling should use POST body, not URL params
owasp_top_10: A03:2021 - Injection (SEC-002 DOM XSS, SEC-009 template injection)

testing_recommendations: Run OWASP ZAP or Burp Suite Community Edition for full penetration testing
Test IDOR: try accessing /api/quotes/999, /invoices/888 with different users
Test XSS: inject <svg onload="alert('xss')"> in email body and view conversation
Test CSRF: create malicious HTML that submits form to /api/quotes/[id]
Test authentication: attempt login brute force, monitor rate limiting
Run npm audit before each deployment
Review all external API integrations for SSRF vulnerabilities
next_steps: 3. Add authorization checks to all resource endpoints (SEC-003)
4. Update dependencies to fix DoS vulnerability (SEC-004)
6. Implement continuous security scanning in CI/CD
7. Consider external penetration testing (OWASP Top 10 validation)
